title: Ginger Chicken Congee
intro: Neil Perry champions gentle, soulful Asian cooking. Congee is the ultimate comfort breakfast, and the Thermomix simmers the rice down to silk while you get on with your morning.
ingredients: Soy, sesame oil, spring onion and coriander, to serve
steps: Place the rice, ginger and stock in the bowl. Cook 40 min / 100°C / Reverse / speed 1, MC off, until the rice breaks down to a porridge.
Add the chicken thigh and cook 12 min / 100°C / Reverse / speed 1.
Shred the chicken, stir back through and season. Serve topped with soy, sesame oil, spring onion and coriander; finish 1 min / 0°C / no speed.
tips: The longer and slower it cooks, the silkier the congee.
Top with anything you love — chilli oil, a soft egg, fried shallots.

faq.a: Time is what breaks the rice down into that signature creamy, soothing porridge.

faq.q: Is this the original Neil Perry recipe?
faq.a: No. This is Chef Aly’s independent Thermomix reinterpretation inspired by Neil Perry and chicken congee; it is not endorsed by or affiliated with the chef.

faq.q: Can I make this Ginger Chicken Congee in a TM6 or TM7?
faq.a: Yes. The method is written with TM6 and TM7 settings, using metric weights, clear times, temperatures and speeds.

description: A Chef Aly Thermomix reinterpretation of Neil Perry-style chicken congee: metric ingredients, exact TM6 and TM7 times, temperatures and speeds for Australian home cooks.
